metadata.title: The Catamaran Crawlers
metadata.source: Catamaran Crawlers premise plus generated System Announcer package
metadata.usage: Regression fixture and prompt baseline for package generation. Not final story prose.
sensitive_portrayal_notes: Kelli's bipolar traits should be written as human character context, not as a joke, diagnosis gimmick, or magical power by itself.
Her risk-taking class rewards volatility and odds-reading in dungeon mechanics while preserving agency, competence, and consequences.
Edward and Kelli should read like specific working-class South Jersey people, not broad regional stereotypes.

premise.logline: Edward and Kelli Marsh sell the house, ghost their kids, and sail out of Atlantic City with Pedro, their 4-year-old macaw, only for the World Dungeon to absorb their catamaran hull-first.
premise.core_tension: Edward retired to stop having tasks. The System is now nothing but tasks. Kelli sees every dungeon risk as a live betting market. Pedro keeps repeating phrases that become mechanically relevant.
premise.book_one_shape: They arrive after crawler factions have already formed. The catamaran becomes a valuable mobile base, Kelli immediately creates political trouble, Edward keeps trying to fix the rigging, and Pedro accidentally reveals how to exploit the first Floor Boss lair.

system_announcer.concept: An ancient, sarcastic administrative entity that has run dungeon absorptions for longer than recorded human history and is professionally exhausted by the paperwork.
voice_pillars: Unable to stop giving status updates

performance.tone: Bureaucratic authority with suppressed exhaustion. Slightly too loud.
performance.delivery: PA system cadence with punchy declarative sentences, brief pauses between status items, and dry emphasis on absurdity.
performance.audio_effect: Light slapback reverb with subtle hall ambience, as if coming from everywhere and nowhere.
emphasis_rules: ALL-CAPS terms get a slight punch.
Quest titles get full delivery weight.
Parenthetical notes are quieter and faster, like fine print being read aloud against the speaker's will.

sample_lines.label: absorption_event
sample_lines.text: ATTENTION REGISTERED VESSEL. You have been successfully integrated into WORLD DUNGEON SECTOR 7, TIER OMEGA, FLOOR ONE. Your catamaran has been flagged as a STRUCTURAL ANOMALY and assigned temporary MOBILE ASSET status. This is unprecedented. Congratulations, I suppose. Please do not do anything with that.

sample_lines.label: edward_profile
sample_lines.text: CRAWLER PROFILE: EDWARD MARSH. Age 62. Class assigned: STRUCTURAL ASSESSOR. Passive ability: Load Bearing Intuition. Active ability: That's Not Up To Code. The System acknowledges your retirement and would like to inform you that retirement is not recognized as a valid exemption from participation. We checked. Twice.

sample_lines.label: kelli_profile
sample_lines.text: CRAWLER PROFILE: KELLI MARSH. Age 61. Class assigned: ALL-IN. Passive ability: Variance Tolerance. Active ability: Double Down. WARNING: Behavioral volatility index is in the 97th percentile of all absorbed crawlers across fourteen civilizations. The System finds this either promising or catastrophic. Probability split is currently 50/50. We will update you.

sample_lines.label: pedro_profile
sample_lines.text: FAMILIAR REGISTERED: PEDRO. Species: Ara chloropterus, green-winged macaw. Age 4. Familiar tier: UNCLASSIFIED. Vocabulary scan: 47 phrases detected. Twelve are usable. Six are concerning. One has been flagged for review by upper administration. Do not teach him anything else.

sample_lines.label: quest_popup
sample_lines.text: NEW QUEST: Don't Die On Floor One. Reward: Continued existence. Difficulty: Should be easy. Historically has not been. Accept / Decline. Note: Decline is not functional. It is included for psychological comfort only.

sample_lines.label: pedro_structural_event
sample_lines.text: FAMILIAR EVENT DETECTED. Pedro has vocalized: THAT'S NOT CODE. Structural weakness identified in Floor Boss lair: NORTHEAST LOAD-BEARING PILLAR. Critical hit modifier applied. The System would like to clarify that it did not plan this and takes no credit. The System is also mildly unsettled.

characters.name: Edward Marsh
characters.narrative_function: Reluctant protagonist and practical problem solver
characters.baseline_voice: Low, tired, dry, pragmatic, and allergic to drama.

mechanics.combat_style: Finds structural failure points and turns the room itself into a weapon.

do: Treat stakes seriously even when the System is absurd.
Use regional, working-person pragmatism instead of heroic speeches.
avoid: Making him wacky just because the dungeon is wacky.
Turning carpentry into generic fantasy magic with no physical logic.

characters.name: Kelli Marsh
characters.baseline_voice: Fast, sharp, funny, fearless when locked in, and impossible to redirect once committed.
core_skills: seeing leverage before other people do

mechanics.combat_style: Turns bad probabilities into forced commitments that can save them or make everything worse.

characters.portrayal_guardrail: Write her volatility with empathy and specificity. The class can reward risk-taking, but bipolar disorder is not the joke and not the power source.
do: Let her be competent, dangerous, loving, and wrong in ways that carry consequences.
Make her betting instinct about pattern recognition and risk appetite.
Let Edward and Kelli argue like people with a long marriage and real history.
avoid: Using mental health language as a punchline.
Making her chaos erase her agency.

familiar.name: Pedro
familiar.baseline_voice: Macaw mimicry, sudden volume spikes, fragments of marital arguments and job-site phrases.
familiar.mechanical_hook: Pedro's learned phrases can trigger System interpretation, identify structural violations, or accidentally satisfy verbal conditions.

home_base.name: The Marsh Catamaran
home_base.function: A surviving home base inside the dungeon, giving the party shelter, storage, repairable systems, and faction leverage.
advantages: Social bargaining chip among crawlers without stable shelter
liabilities: System does not understand how to classify it
Faction leaders want control of it

floor_rules.tone_contract: Characters treat survival seriously while the dungeon systems remain absurd and bureaucratic.
rule_highlights: Registered vessels can be assigned temporary MOBILE ASSET status.
Structural damage can bypass boss defenses if properly justified.
Quest decline buttons may exist for psychological comfort but do not function.
Audience interest affects sponsor attention and loot quality.
floor_rules.first_boss_vulnerability: A lair-specific structural weakness in the northeast load-bearing pillar, discoverable through Edward's assessment and Pedro's phrase trigger.

faction_map.name: The Boardwalk Compact
faction_map.attitude_to_marshes: Wants access to the catamaran and dislikes Kelli immediately.
faction_map.pressure: Political control, protection offers, resource taxation

faction_map.name: The Bilge Rats
faction_map.attitude_to_marshes: View Pedro as a potential mascot and the catamaran as a business opportunity.
faction_map.pressure: Bad deals, information trading, stolen supplies

faction_map.name: The Code Inspectors
faction_map.role: Dungeon cult obsessed with rules and loopholes
faction_map.attitude_to_marshes: Think Edward may be a prophet or a compliance hazard.
faction_map.pressure: Legalistic side quests and dangerous rule exploits

prompt_summary.system: The Interface is a sarcastic administrative PA announcer: bureaucratically enthusiastic, passive-aggressively helpful, professionally exhausted, and obsessed with status updates.
prompt_summary.party: Edward is a retired union carpenter who wants no tasks; Kelli is an odds-reading risk engine with empathy-first portrayal guardrails; Pedro is a macaw familiar whose phrases become mechanics.
prompt_summary.setting: A World Dungeon absorbs an Atlantic catamaran and classifies it as a mobile structural anomaly, making the Marshes useful, targeted, and extremely annoyed.
prompt_summary.tone: Deadly stakes, absurd systems, practical South Jersey/Philadelphia framing, and audio-first punchlines.
